title: Oh, loving wisdom of our God
firstLine: Oh, loving wisdom of our God!
publicDomainBasis: John Henry Newman (1801–1890), John Bacchus Dykes (1823–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Oh, loving wisdom of our God!
When all was sin and shame,
He, the last Adam, to the fight
And to the rescue came.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Oh, wisest love! That flesh and blood
Which did in Adam fail,
Should strive afresh against the foe,
Should strive and should prevail.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
And that a higher gift than grace
Should flesh and blood refine,
God’s presence, and His very self
And essence all-divine.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Oh, generous love! That He, who smote
In man for man the foe,
The double agony in man
For man should undergo.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
And in the garden secretly
And on the cross on high,
Should teach His brethren, and inspire
To suffer and to die.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
As man, yet also God, is He
In our humanity.
The wondrous things He’s done deserve
Our praise eternally.
